member.name: T:ScriptPortal.Vegas.LogFile
member.summary: This is a very simple implementation of a file-based logger.

member.name: M:ScriptPortal.Vegas.LogFile.#ctor
member.summary: Create a new in-memory logger. The log is not written to disk.

member.name: M:ScriptPortal.Vegas.LogFile.#ctor(ScriptPortal.Vegas.Vegas,System.String)
member.summary: Create a new logger. This opens the log file, appending if it already exists.
member.param: the Vegas instance, used to find the application data path
the absolute or relative file path for the log file. If relative, it is combined with the local application data folder of Vegas.

member.name: P:ScriptPortal.Vegas.LogFile.AutoFlush
member.summary: Defaults to true, but you might want to set it to false for speed.

member.name: M:ScriptPortal.Vegas.LogFile.AddLogEntry(System.String)
member.summary: Adds the specified text to the log file. A newline is automatically appended.

member.name: M:ScriptPortal.Vegas.LogFile.Close
member.summary: Closes the output log file. After this call, AddLogEntry() will fail with a null pointer.

member.name: M:ScriptPortal.Vegas.LogFile.ShowLogAsDialog(System.String)
member.summary: Reads in the log file and shows it in a simple scrolling dialog window.

member.name: M:ScriptPortal.Vegas.NestedProject.ReadProject
member.summary: Reads the media file as a Vegas project and returns a new instance. This should be invoked in a "using" block just like Vegas.ReadProject(). If the file cannot be opened, throws an ApplicationException. Multiple calls to this method yield a new instance every time, not the same instance each time.
member.returns: a new Project instance

member.name: M:ScriptPortal.Vegas.NestedProject.ReadProject(ScriptPortal.Vegas.IProgressCallback)
member.summary: Reads the media file as a Vegas project and returns a new instance. This should be invoked in a "using" block just like Vegas.ReadProject(). If the file cannot be opened, throws an ApplicationException. Multiple calls to this method yield a new instance every time, not the same instance each time.
member.param: null or an object to invoke periodically with progress updates
member.returns: a new Project instance

member.name: T:ScriptPortal.Vegas.MediaFiles
member.summary: A immutable collection of the MediaFile assets for a given Media instance. Although this collection is immutable from the scripting API, it is possible that the media may change without warning from the engine side -- for example, if a video proxy is created for the asset that will add a new entry to this collection automatically. This list will not reflect that change until Media.MediaFiles is invoked again.

member.name: T:ScriptPortal.Vegas.MediaFile
member.summary: A file asset associated with a project Media object. Many Media assets have just one file (e.g. Movie.mp4) but in some cases there can be many associated files. For example, Panasonic P2 has separate essence files for video and each audio channel. Most formats support proxy files, and all audio formats have cached peaks files. Some media files store metadata in external sidecar files rather than inside the media file.

member.name: P:ScriptPortal.Vegas.MediaFile.Type
member.summary: The general category of this asset. For example, is it a VideoProxy, or AudioEssence, or Metadata?

member.name: P:ScriptPortal.Vegas.MediaFile.IsProxy
member.summary: True if the file is of type VideoProxy or AudioProxy; otherwise false.

member.name: P:ScriptPortal.Vegas.MediaFile.IsEssence
member.summary: True if the file is of type MixedEssence, VideoEssence, or AudioEssence; otherwise false

member.name: P:ScriptPortal.Vegas.MediaFile.IsMetadata
member.summary: True if the file is of type VegasMetadata or OtherMetadata; otherwise false

member.name: P:ScriptPortal.Vegas.MediaFile.IsRAWCompanion
member.summary: True if the file is of type RAW Companion; otherwise false.

member.name: P:ScriptPortal.Vegas.MediaFile.FilePath
member.summary: The absolute path of this asset.

member.name: P:ScriptPortal.Vegas.MediaFile.PathPattern
member.summary:
A pattern that can be used as the first argument to String.Format to generate the filename for this file asset given the file path of the primary media asset. The subsequence arguments to String.Format should be the directory, the base name, and the extension of the primary asset.
For example, the audio peaks proxy file might have a pattern like "{0}/{1}{2}.sfk". A Panasonic P2 audio essence file might have a pattern like "{0}/../AUDIO/{1}01{2}" indicating the second channel relative to a video file "CONTENTS/VIDEO/0001AB.MXF".

member.name: M:ScriptPortal.Vegas.MediaFile.FormatPath(System.String)
member.summary: Given a path to a primary media asset, calculate a filename for this asset using the PathPattern. This is useful for renaming a group of related files, for example during an archive operation.
member.param: the path to a primary asset.
member.returns: a new path that would be appropriate for this asset given the mediaPath

member.name: M:ScriptPortal.Vegas.MediaFile.FixMetadataFile(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: If you rename a .sfl file, the enclosed reference to the media filename will be wrong. Rewrite the file with the new media filename. This will throw an exception if the metadata file does not exist or if the file is not a metadata file.
member.param: a path to the media file

member.name: T:ScriptPortal.Vegas.MediaPool
member.summary:
Collection of media used in a project.
This pool is represented as an immutable IDictionary, but please note that the IDictionary implementation is incomplete and has flaws that cannot be corrected without breaking backward compatibility. Most significantly: 1) CopyTo, Keys and Values are not implemented, 2) GetEnumerator() iterates over Media items rather than DictionaryEntry items like it's supposed to.
If you try to code "foreach (DictionaryEntry de in pool)" you will get an InvalidCastException. Instead, you need to code as "foreach (Media m in pool)" and the key will be "m.KeyString".

member.name: M:ScriptPortal.Vegas.Project.MatchProjectSettingsWithMedia(ScriptPortal.Vegas.Media)
member.summary: Alters the project's audio and video settings to match the specified media. This is simply shorthand for Video.MatchProjectSettingsWithMedia(media); Audio.MatchProjectSettingsWithMedia(media).
member.param: a non-null media instance that's already added to this project's media pool.
member.returns: true if it succeeds, false if it doesn't due to the file not being valid media or the properties already match

member.name: M:ScriptPortal.Vegas.Project.CreateBackgroundProjectActiveState
member.summary: Call this in a using() block to indicate a block of code during which this project will temporarily be the active project. This should only be used in conjunction with Vegas.CloneProject() or Vegas.CreateEmptyProject(), which enable interaction with background projects. For most of the project properties, you don't need this. But if you want to interact with the BusTracks property then you need to be the active project because only the active project is allowed to communicate with the audio engine.
member.returns: a state object that must be disposed to restore the active project to Vegas.

member.name: M:ScriptPortal.Vegas.Project.UpdateVideoFxUI
member.summary: This will update the VFX UI including the animation timeline

member.name: T:ScriptPortal.Vegas.BackgroundProjectActiveState
member.summary: This class is used just with the Project.CreateBackgroundProjectActiveState() method. Call that method in a using() block to make the specified project temporarily active in Vegas, then when this object is disposed at the end of the block the previously active project will be restored. The main reason why you would use this is to gain access to the BusTracks object, which is only available for the active project. It is legal to nest this call or invoke it on a project that is otherwise already active -- in that case, it just does nothing.

member.name: T:ScriptPortal.Vegas.IProgressCallback
member.summary: Callback object for asynchronous or long-running tasks in Vegas. Several of the scriptable tasks in Vegas, like opening or saving projects, execute on background threads. Some of those allow for periodic progress updates from that thread back to the GUI thread. If your script wants access to this background information, then implement this interface.

member.name: M:ScriptPortal.Vegas.IProgressCallback.UpdatePercentComplete(System.Int32)
member.summary: Invoked on the GUI thread periodically while a background process is running. For a successful operation, this is guaranteed to be called at minimum for 0% and 100% complete. This method might be called more than once for the same percentage, and it's possible for the percentage to go backward (although that should not happen normally).
member.param: a value expected to be in the range of 0 to 100

member.name: T:ScriptPortal.Vegas.VideoAdjustmentEvent
member.summary: Represents a video adjustment event.
member.remarks: Its primary use is for creating new instances of an adjustment event. As it derives from class VideoEvent it inherits most of the methods and properties of VideoEvent. Refer to the description of class VideoEvent for details. There are two significant differences from VideoEvent. VideoAdjustmentEvent has no takes or associated media as it always references the events on tracks below it on the timeline. And, the Name property (inherited from class TrackEvent) is displayed as the event label seen on the timeline. Exception InvalidOperationException is thrown for attempted use of inherited properties or methods which are not applicable to VideoAdjustmentEvent.

member.param: start time of event, or null

member.name: M:ScriptPortal.Vegas.IProjectCOM.ActivateBusTracks(System.Boolean@)
member.summary: Temporarily makes this project into the active project and builds the bus track list. This should be used only in conjunction with Vegas.CloneProject() or Vegas.CreateEmptyProject(). DectivateBusTracks must be invoked to restore the active project to Vegas.

member.name: M:ScriptPortal.Vegas.IProjectCOM.DeactivateBusTracks
member.summary: See ActivateBusTracks.

member.name: M:ScriptPortal.Vegas.IProjectCOM.StartUndoBlock(System.String)
member.summary: Begin an undo scope for this project. Undo blocks cannot be nested.

member.name: M:ScriptPortal.Vegas.IProjectCOM.IsInUndoBlock
member.summary: True if an undo block has been started by StartUndoBlock(). This method does not track undo blocks created deep within Vegas, just the ones initiated by the scripting API.

member.name: M:ScriptPortal.Vegas.IProjectCOM.FinishUndoBlock(System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: End an undo scope. This will fail if there is not an active undo block.

member.name: M:ScriptPortal.Vegas.IProjectCOM.IsModified
member.summary: Checks if the project has been modified since the last save, including calls to the scripting Modified().

member.name: M:ScriptPortal.Vegas.IProjectCOM.Modified
member.summary: Mark the project as modified. This will fail if there is no undo block active. This does not actually modify the project in any way, but helps us know if we need to keep or roll back the last undo block.

member.name: M:ScriptPortal.Vegas.IProjectCOM.CloneProject(System.String,ScriptPortal.Vegas.IProjectProgressUpdateCOM,ScriptPortal.Vegas.IProjectCOM@)
member.summary: Create a new SFPROJECT that is a copy of the current one. The resulting IProjectCOM instance owns that new project and will automatically close it when the IProjectCOM instance is released. If modified, the project is not automatically saved on close, but instead you should invoke IProjectCOM.BeginWrite and IVegasCOM.WaitForIdle before releasing.
member.param: null or the file path where you want this project initially saved. If null, then the project file path points to a non-existent temp file. If not-null, then the specified file will be created as part of the clone process, overwriting any file that might be there.
null or a progress callback that implements IProjectProgressUpdateCOM
a new IProjectCOM instance with the newly instantiated project as its context. The current IProjectCOM and its project is unchanged.

member.name: M:ScriptPortal.Vegas.IProjectCOM.ReadProject(System.String,ScriptPortal.Vegas.IProjectProgressUpdateCOM,ScriptPortal.Vegas.IProjectCOM@)
member.summary: Create a new SFPROJECT by reading the specified project file. The resulting IProjectCOM instance owns that new project and will automatically close it when the IProjectCOM instance is released. If modified, the project is not automatically saved on close, but instead you should invoke IProjectCOM.BeginWrite and IVegasCOM.WaitForIdle before releasing.
member.param: the non-null file path of the project file you wish to read. This file will not be modified by this method.
null or a progress callback that implements IProjectProgressUpdateCOM
a new IProjectCOM instance with the newly instantiated project as its context. The current IProjectCOM and its project is unchanged.

member.name: M:ScriptPortal.Vegas.IProjectCOM.CreateEmptyProject(ScriptPortal.Vegas.IProjectProgressUpdateCOM,ScriptPortal.Vegas.IProjectCOM@)
member.summary: Create a new blank SFPROJECT. The resulting IProjectCOM instance owns that new project and will automatically close it when the IProjectCOM instance is released. If modified, the project is not automatically saved on close, but instead you should invoke IProjectCOM.BeginWrite and IVegasCOM.WaitForIdle before releasing.
member.param: null or a progress callback that implements IProjectProgressUpdateCOM
a new IProjectCOM instance with the newly instantiated project as its context. The current IProjectCOM and its project is unchanged.

member.name: M:ScriptPortal.Vegas.IProjectCOM.FreeClonedProject
member.summary: Unlink the project associated with this instance by CloneProject or CreateEmptyProject. If you fail to call this method, the IProjectCOM destructor will try to free the project but Vegas may have trouble freeing the project at application exit.

member.name: M:ScriptPortal.Vegas.IVegasCOM.SuppressUndoBlockCheck(System.Boolean)
member.summary: This should be used very rarely. If false, then Lock and Modified() will fail without an undo scope. This effect is global, rather than per-project, so use with caution.
